description_long: This church features a tall, square bell tower with a steep, dark green spire, rising above a main rectangular nave. The exterior walls are a light gray stone or plaster, with a series of narrow, vertical windows along the sides and a prominent arched entrance at the base of the tower. The roof is covered in reddish-brown tiles, with multiple gabled sections and a polygonal apse at the rear. The structure is symmetrical, with smaller side entrances and buttresses supporting the main walls. The overall design is simple yet imposing, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on verticality. The church is likely constructed from stone, wood, and tile, with an interior that is mostly hollow to accommodate congregants.
description: A tall, light gray stone church with a square bell tower, steep green spire, and reddish-brown tiled roof. The structure features narrow windows, a main arched entrance, and a polygonal apse at the rear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the tall bell tower with a pointed spire, main arched entrance, and symmetrical side roofs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long nave, series of narrow windows, and the full height of the bell tower.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the polygonal apse, intersecting rooflines, and the back of the bell tower.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible side entrance, buttresses, and the length of the nave.
